response.justification: While the candidate brings 19 years of software engineering experience, extensive agile/XP expertise, and proven mentoring abilities, the role requires deep C++ expertise and industrial cybersecurity knowledge that are not evident in the resume. The candidate's primary experience is in TypeScript/Node.js/web development with only ~6 months of documented C++ work at National Instruments. Critical gaps include no MQTT protocol experience, no IEC 62443 or SDL security standards knowledge, no Azure cloud experience, and missing formal engineering degrees (B.E./M.Tech). For a mid-senior C++ developer role, the technical domain mismatch is too significant.
strengths: 19 years of software development experience exceeding the 8-12 year requirement with staff-level seniority across multiple domains
Strong track record of software craftsmanship, TDD, agile methodologies, and mentoring (workshops, talks, team leadership)
Demonstrated ability to master diverse technology stacks and deliver high-performance systems (async I/O, fibers, real-time processing)
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (only ~6 months at National Instruments); primary expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang—not the core requirement for a C++ Developer role
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity expertise, IEC 62443 standards, Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL), MQTT protocol, or IoT/industrial automation domain knowledge
Missing formal engineering credentials (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech); education is in Computer Science and Fine Arts, not engineering disciplines required by the job specification
